causal_chain.to: Water molecules gain kinetic energy when heated
causal_chain.mechanism: Increased temperature leads to increased molecular motion.

causal_chain.from: Water molecules gain kinetic energy when heated
causal_chain.mechanism: More energetic molecules are able to overcome intermolecular forces and transition to the gaseous phase.

causal_chain.to: Vapor pressure of water equals atmospheric pressure at sea level
causal_chain.mechanism: Heating continues until the vapor pressure matches the external pressure.

causal_chain.from: Vapor pressure of water equals atmospheric pressure at sea level
causal_chain.mechanism: Boiling occurs when the vapor pressure is sufficient to overcome the surrounding atmospheric pressure, allowing bubbles of vapor to form throughout the liquid.

causal_chain.from: Heating water to 100 degrees Celsius at sea level
causal_chain.to: Water molecules gain kinetic energy when heated
causal_chain.mechanism: Temperature is a measure of average kinetic energy of molecules.
